title: 10 Herbal Creams for Quick Relief from Stomach Discomfort and Pain

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for stomach discomfort because it helps to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the stomach, which can ease pain and discomfort caused by cramps, bloating, and nausea.
Shogaol is good for stomach discomfort because it helps to relax the muscles in the stomach, reduce inflammation, and ease cramps, making it a natural remedy for calming an upset stomach.

Zingiberene is good for stomach discomfort because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe the digestive tract, making it easier to manage symptoms such as bloating and cramps.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a natural remedy for stomach discomfort. The cream form of ginger makes it easy to apply and absorbs quickly into the skin. The active ingredient, gingerol, helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce inflammation. This can provide relief from nausea, bloating, and cramping. Ginger has also been shown to slow down digestion, which can help with stomach upset after eating. Additionally, ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm the stomach and reduce irritation. When applied as a cream, the soothing properties of ginger can provide fast relief from stomach discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal creams for stomach discomfort, start by gathering dried or fresh Achillea millefolium flowers, leaves, and stems. You'll need a clean glass jar to dry the herbs, a saucepan, a cheesecloth, a glass measuring cup, and a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Begin by making a herbal infusion: combine 1 cup of dried Achillea millefolium with 2 cups of boiling water in the saucepan. Let it steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the mixture through the cheesecloth into the glass measuring cup. Discard the solids and let the liquid cool. In a separate saucepan, warm 1 cup of carrier oil over low heat. Once the liquid has cooled, slowly pour it into the warm carrier oil, stirring continuously to combine. This is the herbal infused oil. Next, mix 1-2 tablespoons of the herbal infused oil with 1-2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir until the beeswax has melted, then remove from heat and let it cool until it reaches a thick but spreadable consistency. Transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar and let it cool completely before use. This Achillea millefolium herbal cream can be applied topically to the stomach area to help soothe discomfort and inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for stomach discomfort

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for stomach discomfort because it helps soothe the stomach lining, reduces inflammation, and prevents spasms, making it easier to digest food and feel better.

Flavonoids are good for stomach discomfort because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the digestive tract, making them a natural remedy for soothing stomach aches and cramps.
Saponins are good for stomach discomfort because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing irritation and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra cream is a natural remedy for stomach discomfort. It's made from the root of the licorice plant, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When you apply it to your stomach, the cream helps reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive system. This can ease pain and discomfort caused by conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), heartburn, and ulcers. The cream also has a calming effect on the stomach muscles, which can help slow down digestion and prevent stomach cramps. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra cream has antibacterial properties, which can help kill off bad bacteria in the stomach that can cause discomfort. By applying the cream directly to the stomach, you can give your digestive system a much-needed break and start feeling better.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal cream for stomach discomfort, start by gathering dried Achillea millefolium flowers and leaves. You can purchase them from a herbal store or grow them yourself. Next, infuse the herbs in a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, by placing them in a clean glass jar and covering them with the oil. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it every few days. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean jar, discarding the solids. Then, mix the infused oil with a base cream, such as shea butter or beeswax, and a natural emulsifier, like glycerin. Heat the mixture gently until the ingredients are fully incorporated, then let it cool and thicken. Once the cream is ready, transfer it to a clean container and store it in the refrigerator to prolong its shelf life. To use the cream, apply a small amount to the affected area and gently massage it in.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for stomach discomfort

Quercetin helps to reduce stomach discomfort by decreasing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, making it a natural remedy for soothing digestive issues such as bloating and cramps.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, is a natural remedy that can help with stomach discomfort. When peppermint oil is applied to the skin in the form of a cream, it can ease digestive problems. The menthol in peppermint works by relaxing the muscles in the stomach and reducing inflammation. This helps to calm stomach cramps and ease nausea. Peppermint cream can also help to speed up digestion and reduce symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). As it is absorbed into the skin, it can be effective in reducing stomach discomfort without causing any harm. Some people may find it helpful to apply peppermint cream to their abdomen to relieve stomach pain and discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal creams that help with stomach discomfort, start by harvesting fresh or dried yarrow flowers and leaves. Then, combine 1 cup of the yarrow herbs with 2 cups of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a glass jar. Next, place the jar in a saucepan filled with water, and heat it over low heat for 2-3 hours, or until the oil has turned a light green color and has a strong herbal scent. After that, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids. Now, add 1-2 tablespoons of the yarrow oil to a base cream, such as beeswax and coconut oil, and mix well until the beeswax has melted. Pour the mixture into small containers, and let it cool and solidify before use. To apply the cream, gently massage a small amount onto the affected area, and leave it on overnight or until it's absorbed completely. This yarrow cream can help soothe and calm stomach discomfort, while also providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for stomach discomfort

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, is a plant with many health benefits. The creams made from its extracts are good for stomach discomfort because they contain compounds that help relax the muscles in the stomach. These muscles can sometimes get too tight and cause pain, nausea, and vomiting. The fennel creams work by easing this tension and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract. This can help to calm the stomach and relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating. The active ingredients in fennel, such as anethole and estragole, have natural antispasmodic properties, which means they help to stop muscle spasms and relax the stomach. When applied topically as a cream, fennel can be absorbed into the skin and then into the bloodstream, where it can help to soothe the stomach and provide relief from discomfort. Fennel creams may also help to improve digestion and reduce symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal cream for stomach discomfort, start by gathering dried and organic Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers. Combine 2 tablespoons of dried herb with 2 cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant container and let it steep for 10-15 minutes. Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container. Next, combine the herbal infusion with 1 cup of distilled water and 1 tablespoon of vegetable glycerin in a saucepan. Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter, then heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the wax and butter have melted and the mixture has cooled slightly. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool completely before transferring the mixture to a clean glass container. Store the Achillea millefolium herbal cream in the fridge to prevent spoilage and apply it topically to the affected area as needed. It's essential to note that while Achillea millefolium is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, it's not a substitute for medical treatment and should not be used to diagnose or treat underlying medical conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal creams for stomach discomfort

Flavonoids are good for stomach discomfort because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing pain and discomfort caused by stomach ulcers and inflammation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis cream is a natural remedy for stomach discomfort. The main reason is its anti-inflammatory properties. When you apply the cream to your stomach, the anti-inflammatory compounds in it reduce swelling and irritation. This helps to ease the pain and discomfort caused by stomach issues like acid reflux, ulcers, or digestive problems. Additionally, Aloe barbadensis cream has soothing properties that calm the stomach lining, making it less sensitive to irritants. Its antimicrobial properties also prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause infection and discomfort. The cream is also easily absorbed into the skin, allowing it to reach the stomach lining quickly and provide relief from discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal creams that help with stomach discomfort, you will need dried Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl. First, infuse the dried herbs in the carrier oil by placing them in a clean glass jar and letting it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax in the double boiler or in short increments in the microwave until it's fully liquefied. Slowly add the infused oil to the melted beeswax, stirring constantly until it's fully incorporated. Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar or tin, and let it cool and solidify. Once it's set, you can use the Achillea millefolium herbal cream as needed to soothe stomach discomfort by applying a small amount to the affected area.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for stomach discomfort

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis cream is a natural remedy that helps ease stomach discomfort. This cream contains quinine, a substance found in the Cinchona tree. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal cream that helps with stomach discomfort, start by harvesting fresh yarrow flowers, leaves, and stems. Clean and dry them thoroughly, then place them in a clean glass jar and pour vodka or glycerin over the herbs to create a tincture. Let this mixture sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day, to allow the herbs to infuse into the liquid. After the infusion process, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Next, mix 1 cup of the yarrow tincture with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir the mixture until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture is smooth, then remove it from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a consistency similar to thick honey. Finally, pour the yarrow cream into a clean glass jar and store it in the fridge to keep it fresh. To use, simply apply a small amount of the cream to the affected area of your stomach, and let it soothe and calm any discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Peumus boldus cream, also known as boldo cream, is a natural remedy that can help with stomach discomfort. This cream is made from the leaves of the boldo tree, which is native to South America. The tree's leaves have been used for centuries to treat various digestive issues, including indigestion, bloating, and gas. The active compounds in boldo leaves, such as boldine, help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and improve digestion. When applied topically as a cream, it can help to soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation. This can provide quick relief from discomfort and help to prevent stomach problems from getting worse. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal cream for stomach discomfort, first, you'll need some dried Achillea millefolium flowers and leaves, which can be found in most health food stores or online. Start by making a herbal infusion by steeping 2 tablespoons of the dried herbs in 2 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes. Let it cool and then strain it. Next, mix 1/2 cup of a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, with the cooled herbal infusion in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir occasionally until the mixture reaches a temperature of 120-130 degrees Fahrenheit, then remove it from the heat and let it cool. Once it has cooled to room temperature, whip it with a hand mixer or whisk until it becomes light and fluffy. Finally, add a thickening agent like beeswax or candelilla wax to help it solidify into a cream. Stir well and pour it into a container. Let it cool and harden completely before using it on your skin to help soothe stomach discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Carum carvi cream, also known as caraway seed cream, has been used for centuries to help with stomach discomfort. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal cream for stomach discomfort, you'll need dried Achillea millefolium flowers, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a few simple ingredients for the cream base. First, steep 1 tablespoon of dried Achillea millefolium flowers in 2 tablespoons of carrier oil for 2-3 weeks, shaking the container daily, to create a potent herbal infusion. After the infusion is ready, strain it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids. Then, melt 1-2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals, stirring between each interval. Next, mix the melted beeswax with 2-3 tablespoons of the herbal infusion and 2-3 tablespoons of a cream base, such as shea butter or coconut cream, until well combined. Finally, pour the mixture into a small tin or jar, let it cool and harden, and your Achillea millefolium herbal cream is ready to use. remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa cream is made from turmeric, a plant with natural healing powers. The main ingredient in turmeric is a chemical called curcumin. This curcumin is very good at reducing swelling and pain in the body. When you have stomach discomfort, it's often because your stomach is inflamed or swollen. Curcumin in the cream helps to calm down the inflammation and reduce the pain. It also helps to protect the stomach lining from damage. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal cream that helps with stomach discomfort, you'll need dried Yarrow flowers and a few basic ingredients. First, infuse the dried Yarrow flowers in a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, by placing them in a clean glass jar and leaving it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. This process allows the plant's active properties to dissolve into the oil. Next, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container, discarding the solids. To make the cream, mix the infused oil with a thickening agent like beeswax and shea butter, melting them together in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Once the mixture is smooth and melted, remove it from the heat and let it cool slightly before whipping it with a hand mixer or whisk until it thickens and becomes creamy. Transfer the cream to a clean container and store it in the fridge, where it will keep for up to 6 months. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal creams for stomach discomfort, start by gathering a handful of dried Achillea millefolium flowers and leaves. Combine the dried herbs with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a small saucepan. intro_desc: Herbal creams can provide relief from stomach discomfort by using natural ingredients that soothe and calm the digestive system. Two herbs commonly used in these creams are Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, and Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea and vomiting, while also easing digestion and reducing pain in the stomach. Licorice root, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort.  Stomach discomfort can be a debilitating issue that affects daily life, making it difficult to eat, sleep, and even perform simple tasks. If left unchecked, it can lead to more serious health problems like acid reflux, stomach ulcers, and even mental health issues like anxiety and depression. Fixing stomach discomfort is crucial because it can significantly improve quality of life. By using herbal creams that contain ginger and licorice root, individuals can experience relief from stomach discomfort, allowing them to enjoy their favorite foods, engage in physical activities, and even sleep better at night. This can have a ripple effect, improving mood, energy levels, and overall well-being.
supplementary_best: For stomach discomfort, the best combination of herbal creams would be a blend of 'Glycyrrhiza glabra' (licorice root), 'Carum carvi' (caraway seeds), and 'Mentha x piperita' (peppermint). 'Glycyrrhiza glabra' helps to soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and irritation. 'Carum carvi' seeds contain compounds that ease digestive issues and reduce spasms, allowing food to move through the digestive system smoothly. 'Mentha x piperita' peppermint oil has natural anti-inflammatory properties that ease cramps and discomfort in the stomach. When combined, these herbs work together to provide fast relief from stomach discomfort, reducing inflammation, soothing the stomach lining, and easing digestive issues.
supplementary_avoid: If you have stomach discomfort, there are some herbal creams you should avoid. Capsicum cream contains capsaicin, which can irritate your stomach lining and make your discomfort worse. It's also found in chili peppers, so it's best to steer clear of any products with capsaicin.  Senna cream is another one to watch out for. Senna is a plant that's used as a laxative, and applying it to your skin can cause stomach cramps and diarrhea. This is because senna can increase bowel movements and make your stomach work harder.  Ginkgo biloba cream is not recommended for people with stomach issues. While ginkgo biloba is often used to improve memory and circulation, it can also cause stomach upset and nausea. If you're already experiencing discomfort, ginkgo biloba cream may make things worse.  Comfrey cream may also not be suitable for people with stomach problems. Comfrey contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which can be toxic to your liver and cause stomach pain.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera juice is a natural remedy that can help with stomach discomfort. Drinking aloe vera juice after eating can help soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the stomach. This is because aloe vera contains compounds that can calm the digestive tract and prevent irritation. Some people find that drinking aloe vera juice helps to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, such as bloating and cramps.  Ginger is another natural remedy that can help with stomach discomfort. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach and alleviate nausea and vomiting. Ginger can be consumed in many ways, including as tea, capsules, or added to food. Many people find that ginger helps to soothe the stomach and reduce symptoms of indigestion and bloating.  Probiotics are live bacteria and yeast that can help maintain a healthy balance of gut bacteria. When the balance of gut bacteria is disrupted, it can lead to stomach discomfort, bloating, and other digestive issues. Probiotics can be found in fermented foods such as yogurt, kefir, and sauerkraut, or can be taken as a supplement. Consuming probiotics can help to restore the balance of gut bacteria and alleviate symptoms of stomach discomfort.  Apple cider vinegar has been used for centuries as a natural remedy for digestive issues. Apple cider vinegar contains acetic acid, which can help stimulate digestion and reduce symptoms of bloating and gas. Apple cider vinegar can be consumed as a drink or added to food, but it's often recommended to start with small amounts and gradually increase the dose as the body adjusts. Some people find that apple cider vinegar helps to alleviate symptoms of indigestion and heartburn.  Bentonite clay is a natural remedy that can help absorb toxins and reduce inflammation in the stomach. Bentonite clay can be consumed as a powder or added to water to make a drink. Some people find that bentonite clay helps to alleviate symptoms of bloating and gas, and can also help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.  Slippery elm is a natural remedy that can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. Slippery elm contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that can help calm the digestive tract and prevent irritation. Some people find that slippery elm helps to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome and can also help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.  Chamomile tea can also help with stomach discomfort. Chamomile contains compounds that can help calm the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. Drinking chamomile tea after eating can help soothe the stomach and reduce symptoms of indigestion and bloating. Some people find that chamomile tea helps to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome and can also help to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience stomach discomfort may also have other health issues. For example, irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) can cause stomach pain, bloating, and changes in bowel movements. The medicinal herb Peppermint can help with IBS symptoms by relaxing the muscles in the stomach and reducing inflammation. Ginger, another herb, has anti-inflammatory properties that can ease nausea and stomach pain.  Some people with stomach discomfort may also have acid reflux, a condition where stomach acid flows back up into the esophagus, causing heartburn and chest pain. Licorice root has been shown to help protect the mucous lining in the stomach, reducing acid reflux symptoms.  Additionally, stomach discomfort can be linked to stress and anxiety. Chamomile, a calming herb, can help reduce stress and promote relaxation, which can in turn alleviate stomach discomfort.  Stomach discomfort can also be a sign of an underlying digestive issue, such as small intestine bacterial overgrowth (SIBO). The medicinal herb Berberine has been shown to help reduce SIBO symptoms by killing off bad bacteria in the gut.  Lastly, stomach discomfort can be a symptom of an underlying issue with the gut microbiome, such as low stomach acid. The medicinal herb Betaine HCl can help stimulate stomach acid production, which can aid in digestion and alleviate stomach discomfort.
